hero_metrics.subtext: Share of simulated outcomes with negative NPV.

hero_metrics.subtext: Deterministic downside case aligned with a tougher investment committee lens.

benchmark.label: Incremental NPV
benchmark.reconciliation_note: Workbook deterministic VAN for the incremental expansion flow.

benchmark.label: Incremental IRR
benchmark.reconciliation_note: Workbook deterministic IRR for the incremental expansion flow.

benchmark.label: Expected NPV
benchmark.reconciliation_note: Workbook Monte Carlo expected NPV for the incremental expansion model. Comparison blocked by: stochastic_design_mismatch, basis_mismatch.

benchmark.label: VaR 5%
benchmark.reconciliation_note: Workbook 5th percentile NPV for the incremental expansion model. Comparison blocked by: stochastic_design_mismatch, basis_mismatch.

benchmark.label: CVaR 5%
benchmark.reconciliation_note: Workbook conditional tail mean beyond the 5th percentile for the incremental expansion model. Comparison blocked by: stochastic_design_mismatch, basis_mismatch.

insights.label: Value Creation
insights.title: The base case is positive, but not bulletproof.
insights.body: Base NPV is 1.74B USD and the project pays back in year 4. That is enough to justify attention, but not enough to skip downside framing.

insights.label: Tail Risk
insights.title: The left tail is the real portfolio story.
insights.body: Loss probability is 31.0%. The 5% VaR is -2.54B USD and the CVaR is -3.30B USD, which means adverse combinations are economically severe.

insights.label: Decision Signal
insights.title: Scenario spread reveals investment fragility.
insights.body: The strongest deterministic case is Bull Market at 5.22B USD, while Investment Committee Downside falls to -7.21B USD. The committee-downside case alone reaches -7.21B USD.
